questions.question: A cargo ship has $3$ containers of diesel fuel and $5$ containers of gasoline. If $2$ more containers of diesel fuel are added, what is the new ratio of diesel fuel to gasoline?

A freeway has a $15$-mile stretch with a speed limit of $65$ miles per hour. If $20%$ of the road is under construction, reducing the speed limit to $45$ miles per hour, what is the average speed for the entire stretch?
A package delivery company has $120$ vans, with $30%$ of them being electric. If they add $25$ more electric vans, what percentage of the total vans will be electric?

A train travels from City A to City B at an average speed of $60$ miles per hour, covering $30%$ of the total distance. If it then travels at $80$ miles per hour for the remaining distance, what is the average speed for the entire trip?

A logistics company has $18$ trucks, with $40%$ of them being used for international shipping. If $3$ more trucks are added for domestic shipping, what percentage of the total trucks will be used for domestic shipping?
A bike-sharing company has $50$ bicycles, with $20%$ of them being electric. If they add $10$ more bicycles, $60%$ of which are electric, what percentage of the total bicycles will be electric?

A bus travels from City A to City B, covering $40%$ of the total distance at an average speed of $50$ miles per hour. If it then travels at $70$ miles per hour for the remaining distance, what is the average speed for the entire trip?

A shipping company has $200$ containers, with $25%$ of them being refrigerated. If they add $50$ more containers, $40%$ of which are refrigerated, what percentage of the total containers will be refrigerated?

A transportation company has $x$ vans and $y$ trucks. The vans travel at an average speed of $40$ miles per hour, while the trucks travel at an average speed of $60$ miles per hour. If the company wants to increase the average speed of the entire fleet by $10%$, how many more vans or trucks should they add? Assume the total number of vehicles is $x + y = 100$. Show your work and calculate the number of additional vehicles needed.

A delivery company has a $15$-mile route with $3$ traffic lights. The first traffic light is $3$ miles from the starting point, the second is $5$ miles from the first, and the third is $7$ miles from the second. If the delivery truck travels at $30$ miles per hour and stops for $2$ minutes at each traffic light, what is the average speed for the entire route? Show your work and calculate the average speed.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Make sure students show their work and calculations for each problem.
Tip 2: Encourage students to use diagrams and visual aids to help solve the problems.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their units and ensure they are consistent throughout the problem.
